TextENGNew: He once described believing as staking a coin named hope.
changeReason: Old 'Someone once suggested' vs new 'He once described'; JPN 彼はたとえた = 'he likened it.' Old uses vague 'someone,' new correctly says 'he.'

TextENGNew: In other words... to believe is to accept risk.
Win and you gain something; lose and you lose something.
As with most gambles, we search for a sure thing before laying our coin down.
But as with most gambles, there is no sure thing.
Sometimes you win, sometimes you lose.
And in that way, the coins of hope accumulate.
changeReason: Old 'increase the number of coins you have' drops the qualifier; JPN 希望というコイン = 'coins of hope.' New preserves this important metaphor.

TextENGNew: But when the losses pile up, you begin to dread the next bet.
...And then it dawns on you.
If you stake nothing, you lose nothing.

To stake nothing is to believe in nothing.
To believe in nothing is to involve yourself in nothing.
To involve yourself in nothing is the same as not existing.
And so she "was not."
changeReason: Old 'she didn't exist' vs new 'she "was not"'; JPN uses quotation marks around いなかった, emphasizing the philosophical distinction that the new translation preserves.

TextENGNew: The one coin remaining in her chest was the last.
Lose it, and she would vanish.
...That fear was why she had never stopped trembling at the thought of betting.
She had kept running — trying not to get entangled in any contest, terrified of staking it by accident.
But just as in a game of poker,
...even folding costs you a coin.
In trying not to lose her last coin... she had been spending it away, one slow moment at a time.
And there was one more thing she did not know.
...No — I did not know it either.
The more coins poured into the pot... the greater our odds of winning this game.
In other words, the more frightened we remain, the more we lose the game set before us.
It is just like carrying a portable shrine.
No single person can lift such a thing alone.
But when many join their strength, it rises with astonishing ease.
The line between too heavy to lift and effortlessly aloft is surely a sharp one.
Like the tipping point of a balanced scale, tilted by the faintest added weight.
The great, heavy shrine before us — no matter how we tried, it would not rise.
Everyone strained to shoulder it, and yet it would not move.
I watched and told myself it was pointless, so I did not lend my hands.
I told myself I could not win, so I would not enter.
I would not stake my coin.
...Yes.
That was wrong.
If I had added my shoulder to the rest, perhaps the shrine would have lifted easily.
For so long I had played the bystander.
I had mistaken myself for a mere observer — someone who only watched to see whether the odds were worth betting on.
But that was wrong!
I am not a bystander, nor should I ever be.
I should have joined in!
The game before me looks, at first glance, like one I cannot win.
At first glance it seems not worth the wager.
But by betting on it — by joining, by believing — the outcome might yet change!

To stake something is to believe in it.
To believe in something is to be involved in it.
To be involved in something is to exist.
And so she, at last... "was."
changeReason: Old 'she existed' vs new 'she, at last... "was"'; JPN uses quotes around いた and 今こそ = 'at last/now,' emphasizing the philosophical weight.

TextENGNew: "All right, everyone — morning homeroom is starting!! No more chatter! Class rep, call it!"
"Rise! Bow!"
"Good morning!"
"Be seated!"
Scrape, scrape, scrape, scrape.
changeReason: Old 'Rattle, rattle, rattle' vs new 'Scrape, scrape, scrape'; JPN ガタガタ is the sound of chairs scraping, not rattling.

TextENGNew: It was an ordinary morning.
The only difference was that it started just a little late.
Chie-sensei, a creature of clockwork habit, always arrived in the classroom exactly on time — but today, unusually, she was a fraction behind.
Why was homeroom running late?
The rumours had already swept the room before the teacher arrived.
When the teacher called out toward the hallway, a girl peeked in, hesitant and uncertain.
"...Au au au..."
"This is our new transfer student, Hanyuu Furude-san. She will be joining us starting today."
"Whoaaa!!!"
A wave of welcome and wonder and delight rose from every corner of the classroom.
"Furude? Wait, is she related to Rika-chan?!"
"...That is right.@k@vS13/05/DS41050004. She is a distant relative of mine."
"My, I was quite surprised too! I had no idea Rika had a relative!"
"Well, even I had no idea! The three families can get rather complicated, after all."
"But she really does look like Rika-chan's relative! Hauu~ she's so adorable!!"
"...Au au au..."
The excitement of the class was something else.
The new transfer student looked a little overwhelmed by it all.
Rika-chan has a kind of calm, at-her-own-pace quality to her, but this Hanyuu girl seems to be exactly the opposite.
Timid, you might say — small-animal energy.
...In other words, precisely the type that sets Rena's instincts blazing.
...Look at her.
Rena's already plotting how to swallow her whole and carry her home...
"Now then, Hanyuu-san, please introduce yourself. Go right ahead."
The teacher stepped aside and beckoned her to the center.
The silence seemed to press in on her, and she stood fidgeting for a long moment.
...Well, I get it.
This kind of thing is nerve-wracking.
"...Um... au au... I'm Hanyuu Furude."
"...Mii! She is my relative. I hope everyone will be kind to her."
Rika-chan clearly understood better than anyone how nervous Hanyuu was about introducing herself.
Right.
It would be cruel to let the silence press down on her like that.
So we decided to liven things up right away.
"You share the name Furude with Rika-chan! Hau, now there are two Furudes."
"Then let's just call her Hanyuu. If we say Furude, Rika will turn around too, won't she?"
Using first names freely among ourselves was our unspoken rule.
She was one of us now — she deserved to be brought in.
"Then it's decided! From today we'll call you Hanyuu! I'm Mion Sonozaki, class rep! Call me Mion!"
"Ohohoho!! I am Satoko Houjou! So pleased to meet you, Hanyuu-san!"
"Um! Do you like sports? We have a match next week — please come watch!"
"Have you seen much of the village yet? If there's anything you need to know, just ask me!"
"Hello~! Let's be friends! What year are you in?"
One after another, hands shot up, and the introductions tumbled over each other in a rush.
A charming eagerness filled the room — everyone wanted to be remembered, to be the one who became a new friend.
I had only just transferred in myself not long ago, and it had been just like this — warm and unhurried.
I remember how grateful I was, all nerves, that everyone was so welcoming.
I should know better than anyone how a transfer student feels — I just went through it.
I had better do my part too!
"Right. Let's help her settle in as quickly as possible."
"Right, Rena transferred last year, didn't she? Were you nervous when you came?"
"Yeah. But Mii-chan and everyone were so kind, I fit in right away."
"Well, naturally! Welcoming newcomers is important work for the three families."
"You only invoke the three families when it's convenient for you. But yeah, it's true that even Mion helped people fit in."
"Then it's Kei-chan's turn to look after someone now, isn't it?"
"You're right! Rena was a transfer student too, and she still looked after me when I arrived after her. So now it's my turn to be kind to the new one!"
One new transfer student, and the whole atmosphere of the classroom shifts like this.
I even feel like my own way of thinking has changed.
We decided right away to surround Hanyuu at lunch.
"All right, everyone!! One question per person for Hanyuu! Let's get right to the heart of things — who's going first?!"
"Um, um, um! What's your favourite food?!"
"...Au, I love all sweet things. My absolute favourite is cream puffs."
"Oh, how wonderful!! I love them too! There's a delicious pastry shop in Okinomiya. I shall take you there sometime."
"Did you hear that, boys?! If you want to earn Hanyuu's approval, cream puffs are the answer! Not cheap ones — the expensive kind from a fancy shop is what counts!"
"Now, who's next?! If nobody steps up, I'll start asking about her type~!"
changeReason: Old asks 'what color her underwear is' vs new asks 'about her type'; JPN 好きな子のタイプ = 'type of person she likes,' not underwear.

TextENGNew: "...Au au au!"
Hanyuu was overwhelmed, bombarded with questions from all sides.
An initiation rite for transfer students, I suppose.
I remember my first day going just like this.
Mion and Satoko ganging up on her too — same as they did to me.
I glanced at Rena and saw a look of quiet emotion on her face.
Hers had probably been the same.
"Rena, are you thinking back to your own first day?"
"...Eh? ...Oh, yeah. Ahahahaha."
"Were you thinking about something else? Already plotting how to take Hanyuu home, I bet."
"W-hauu?! I'm not thinking anything like that~!"

"I'm not at all thinking about how Hanyuu-chan said she loves cream puffs, or how Rena happens to have delicious cream puffs at home, or how I could invite her over — not thinking about it at all~?! Hauu~!! Taking her home~!!"
"Ahahahahahaha, if she suddenly stops coming to school starting tomorrow, we'll know exactly who's responsible!"
"Hau, that's terrible~ ahahahaha."
We laughed together, Rena and I, watching the tender scene between Hanyuu and her new classmates.
"...Hey, Keiichi-kun. ...Somehow... I'm so glad."
"So am I. There's nothing quite like welcoming a new classmate."
"Um... it's not really that... I feel like I've seen this girl somewhere before."
"Hanyuu? Maybe when she came to look around before moving? You have a good memory, Rena."
"...Mm... it's not quite like that... Hanyuu-chan feels like she was around much longer — like she was always right nearby, watching all of us playing together. ...For so, so long."
"...Hm? Just me? You noticed too, didn't you, Keiichi-kun?"
"...No, I've never seen her before. Are you saying you know her?"
Going by how Mion and Satoko reacted, Hanyuu was a complete stranger to them.
If she had been right nearby while we played, as Rena was suggesting, surely someone would remember.
"You're imagining things. First of all, she's a transfer student — she can't have been around the village that often."
"...Mmmm... maybe? I feel like I did see her though..."
"You're imagining it, imagining it. Hanyuu just said a moment ago she used to live far away before transferring."
"...Mmm... but Rena really did see her... hau."
Rena remained unconvinced, searching through the blurry edges of her memory.
Déjà vu, most likely.
That feeling of already having seen something you are encountering for the first time.
"Doesn't really matter, does it? She's here now."
"That's true. Yeah. ...She always looked so eager to join our circle... so I'm just glad she finally has. ...Ahahahaha."
For some reason, Rena smiled with something like tears in her eyes.
I glanced over and noticed Rika-chan wore the same expression.
I didn't understand why the two of them would look like that.
That faint puzzlement was swept away quickly enough — the moment Satoko descended on us and the noise began.
"Gooooood byeeee!!!"
Of all the words exchanged in a school day, none feel quite as good as those.
The moment class ended, the students poured out of the room in a rushing flood.
Hanyuu had been the class darling for the whole day.
Being roughly the same age as Rika-chan and the others, she had been surrounded by the younger students all day, and those of us in the upper grades — Mion and I — had barely managed to get near her.
"Well, from the class's perspective, a cute transfer student their own age is a lot more exciting than some scary upperclassman arriving."
"Ahahahaha. You did look a bit scary at first, Keiichi-kun — you seemed so tense."
"Come on, that's not fair?! I was trying to be friendly and settle in quickly!"
"Where exactly was that friendly?! You gave me a flick on the forehead on my very first day after transferring in!"
"That was because my classroom debut was sabotaged with a trap! And there were thumbtacks on the chair and a frog in the desk!"
"...When I heard a transfer student was in the staff room and went to see, Keiichi looked extremely nervous. So Satoko must have decided to help him relax."
"Ohohoho! I was being considerate, as a senior member of this community!"
"Oh, that is absolutely not what happened!"
...That said. Truth be told, that blackboard-eraser trap had actually worked to ease my nerves.
But I was not about to admit that in front of Satoko.
It would only make her insufferable.
"Hanyuu-chan went home with everyone else. She must be the most fought-over playmate in the village by now."
"Ahahahaha. But the first day really is exhausting. She'll probably be flat out the moment she gets home."
"Quite so. Ohohoho!"
"Now then, everyone~!! My part-time work kept me away for ages — the club is back in session!!"
"I've been waiting and waiting~!! I'm going to get rusty at this rate!"
"...Mii! If no one gets punished soon, I'll grow bored too."
"Hauu~! Rena won't lose. I haven't played in ages, so no mercy~!"
"Kuhkuhkuh! Everyone's been storing up energy for quite a while... how delightful. I wouldn't enjoy crushing people who weren't properly raring for a fight!!"
"Heh! You're all fired up. But I'm in top form today!! You should have stayed at your part-time job, Mion — you'll be sorry you came!!"
Apparently there had been a staff shortage at Mion's uncle's shop, and Mion had been kept busy with shifts for some time now.
So this was our first club meeting in ages.
For us club members, the main reason to come to school at all is the club.
In a certain sense, the day only really begins when the afternoon session starts.
And if there is no club to look forward to, coming to school feels almost pointless.
So every single one of us was vibrating with suppressed excitement at the long-overdue gathering.
"At this rate, a half-hearted game won't slake everyone's thirst... kuhkuhkuh! Now, what shall I cook up for you all today...!!"
Mion started rummaging through the club locker.
...I always wonder what she has in there and how much of it.
I'm fairly certain there's more packed inside than the locker's interior volume should allow.
Just then, the classroom door slid open with a clatter.
"...Huh? Hanyuu-chan?"
"...Au, ...h-hello."
"Oh, what happened? Did you forget something?"
It was Hanyuu — I had thought she had gone home long ago.
Maybe she forgot her lunchbox.
You can manage without a textbook, but forgetting your lunchbox is another matter.
"...Au au au au..."
"Hm? Hanyuu, what's wrong? Looking for something?"
She was watching us with an apologetic, almost guilty look.
...What was going on?
Are we in the way somehow?
If she needed something, she could just come out and say it, but instead she stood there fidgeting, timidly staring at us.
Hm?
Wait, are we sitting in Hanyuu's seat?
No, that's not it.
``What is it, Hanyuu-chan...?@k@vS13/02/DS41020054. Is something troubling you? Is it?``
Rena tried talking to her, but no real communication was taking place.
It was almost like trying to have a conversation with a stray cat.
...What on earth is going on?
Hanyuu was staring fixedly at something.
...When I followed her gaze to see what she was looking at, it turned out to be Rika-chan.
...Hanyuu had been watching Rika-chan the whole time, with an apologetic look on her face.
``...I won't say a thing.@k@vS13/05/DS41050015. If Hanyuu wants it, she should say it with her own mouth.``
I was finally starting to grasp the situation.
Hanyuu wanted to say something, but couldn't put it into words, so she was hoping Rika-chan would speak on her behalf.
But Rika-chan was refusing, telling her to say it herself.
In that case, we would have to hear what Hanyuu was actually trying to say.
We all waited quietly, watching for Hanyuu to speak up.
...But come to think of it, it was already clear from this morning's self-introduction that Hanyuu is terrible with this kind of silence.
Rena picked up on that right away and gently spoke to her.
``What's wrong, Hanyuu-chan?@k@vS13/02/DS41020056. You can whisper it to Rena.@k@vS13/02/DS41020057. I'll hear you out, okay...?``
``Hm?@k@vS13/02/DS41020059. What was that?@k@vS13/02/DS41020060. Could you say that one more time?``
``...Au au au au.``
Rena let out a startled yelp.
``What is it? Tell me too.@k@vS13/03/DS41030042. What's going on?``
``Uh, um... are you sure it's okay?!``
``What seems to be the matter?@k@vS13/04/DS41040026. All that mumbling is quite unsettling.``
``Um, well, you see...!``
``...I... I want to join your club...!``
Hanyuu blurted it out in a loud voice, as though forcing herself past her own embarrassment.
``Ha, hahahahahahaha!!@k@vS13/03/DS41030045. Now that's a surprise!@k@vS13/03/DS41030046. Where on earth did she hear about us?``
``Au au au...!@k@vS13/12/DS41070017. I want to join...@k@vS13/12/DS41070018. I want to be part of the club...``
``...Good job saying it yourself.@k@vS13/05/DS41050017. Clap, clap, clap.``
Rika-chan had known all along that Hanyuu wanted to join the club.
Mion grinned and folded her arms, staring Hanyuu down as if appraising her, needlessly making her shrink back.
``...Hmmmm, so there's a fearless soul who actually wants to throw herself into the jaws of death.@k@vS13/03/DS41030048. ...But our club isn't just some casual little playgroup.``
``Only the chosen, after surviving grueling trials, are permitted to join this glorious club.@k@vS13/03/DS41030050. You can't just waltz in with a 'please let me join' and get a 'sure, why not'...!``
``Um, Hanyuu-chan...@k@vS13/02/DS41020066. Mii-chan's club might look really fun from the outside, but...@k@vS13/02/DS41020067. well... hau, it's actually pretty intense, you know?``
``...Au, I know what I'm getting into...``
``Winning is expected.@k@vS13/04/DS41040029. If you lose, something terrible will happen to you.@k@vS13/04/DS41040030. Are you truly prepared for that?!``
``...Au, I won't lose...@k@vS13/12/DS41070021. ...A-And besides...``
At that, Hanyuu swallowed hard and said what must have been the thing she most wanted to say.
For her, it was remarkably eloquent... and carried what felt like a deep, profound meaning.
``I used to run away from every contest because I couldn't bear to lose...@k@vS13/12/DS41070023. But... running away is even worse than losing...``
``Only by enduring the pain of defeat and continuing to fight can one finally achieve victory... That is what I was taught.``
``...Hanyuu.``
She may have looked timid on the surface, but... when Mion saw that Hanyuu had genuine backbone, she broke into a delighted grin.
She had clearly taken a liking to her.
``Well, well.@k@vS13/01/DS41010043. She's got more guts than I expected!@k@vS13/01/DS41010044. What do you say, Mion? Why not give her a shot right here?@k@vS13/01/DS41010045. An entrance exam!``

``Hmmmm... heh heh heh!@k@vS13/03/DS41030052. The entrance exam that normally requires weeks of observation before you even qualify to take it, and she wants to do it on her very first day as a transfer student...@k@vS13/03/DS41030053. Now what should I do about this, heh heh heh!``
Mion was dragging things out as dramatically as possible, but she was just toying with her.
If she truly didn't intend to let Hanyuu join, she would have turned her away at the door from the start.
After all, if this level of intimidation was enough to make someone falter, they'd never survive in our club.

``As Rena and Satoko just told you,@k@vS13/03/DS41030055. our club is strict!@k@vS13/03/DS41030056. Winning is expected.@k@vS13/03/DS41030057. The only goal is first place.@k@vS13/03/DS41030058. And if you're unlucky enough to come in last, you'll face a punishment so terrible you'll regret ever being born!``
``...Au au au!@k@vS13/12/DS41070026. But I'll do my best...``
``Ahahahaha.@k@vS13/02/DS41020069. But the penalty games are actually pretty fun too.``
After all, you've been watching for so long, haven't you, Hanyuu-chan?
...For so, so long, wanting to join the group, just watching.
...I felt as though that was what Rena had whispered.
``Come on, Mion, quit stalling and just test her already.@k@vS13/01/DS41010047. I went through the same thing when I joined, didn't I?``
``Indeed.@k@vS13/04/DS41040032. Let us see what Hanyuu-san is made of!``
``Ahaha, do your best, Hanyuu-chan!@k@vS13/02/DS41020071. Rena won't go easy on you!``
``...I-I won't go easy either...!``
``...I won't go easy on Hanyuu either.``
``...Au au au au!``
``All right, I like the look of you!!@k@vS13/03/DS41030060. Hanyuu Furude, you are hereby permitted to take the entrance exam for our club!!``

``Well then, that changes things!@k@vS13/03/DS41030062. We'll need to pick a game Hanyuu can follow...@k@vS13/03/DS41030063. Hmm, what would be good...?@k@|@y@vS13/03/DS41030064. ...Oh...! Heh heh heh!@k@vS13/03/DS41030065. How about this one?``
What Mion slammed down onto the middle of the desk with exaggerated flair was a deck of playing cards.
Which means... it's going to be that, isn't it?!
``Let's go with a game everyone knows!!@k@vS13/03/DS41030067. How about Old Maid?!``
``...Au, b-bring it on...!``
Just as I feared -- she'd brought out the very same marked deck that was used on me during my own entrance exam...!
``Oh ho ho ho!@k@vS13/04/DS41040034. What a spirited response!@k@vS13/04/DS41040035. But... our club's Old Maid is a bit different, you know?``
You see, almost every card in the deck has scratches on the back, so by memorizing those scratches, you can tell what card it is without seeing the face.
In other words, the more experience you have playing with this deck, the stronger you are -- making it the perfect game for hazing newcomers...!
``H-Hanyuu-chan, good luck...@k@vS13/02/DS41020073. This is your trial... hau...``
``...Go for it, yay.``
How nostalgic.
I went through this entrance exam too.
It was only a short while ago, but somehow it makes me feel strangely sentimental.

``But!@k@vS13/01/DS41010049. That's a completely separate matter from going easy on her.@k@vS13/01/DS41010050. Just like everyone crushed me without mercy back then!!@k@vS13/01/DS41010051. I'm going to crush her without mercy too!@k@vS13/01/DS41010052. Right, everyone?!``
``Prepare yourself, Hanyuu-san!!!``
``Here we go!!@k@vS13/03/DS41030069. If you want a seat at the very bottom of this club, prove you're worthy!!!``
``Heh heh heh!@k@vS13/03/DS41030071. I'll call your hand from right to left!@k@vS13/03/DS41030072. Ace, three, four, seven, Jack, Queen!!``
``It's too late to cover them with your hand now!!``
``Au au au au!!``
``Sorry, Hanyuu-chan -- this is the Ace of Spades, right?@k@|@y@vS13/02/DS41020075. I'm done!``
"Wahahahaha, how does it feel, Hanyuu?! Welcome to the trial by fire!!"
"...Mii. Keiichi's grudge from his own admission trial is showing."
"Now now, Kei-chan, is that the time for that? Hanyuu, you can win with Kei-chan's right card."
"...Au! I win!"
"Uwaaaa, that's cheating~!!"
"Right, I'm going serious now! How dare you make me angry!! Hanyuu, do you want to crawl home on all fours wearing a collar and a tail sticking out of bloomers on your very first day?!"
"Oh my! That description suits you far better, Keiichi-san!!"
"Hauu~, yes yes!! And Rena will take that home!!"
"I-I'll give Keiichi the most embarrassing penalty game too...!"
changeReason: New adds 'most embarrassing' from JPN 恥ずかしい罰ゲーム; old just says 'that punishment.'

TextENGNew: "Kuhkuhkuh! What a splendid idea — count me in!!"
"Wh-when did the whole room turn against me?! Fine then!! I'll make every last one of you crawl home on all fours!!"
changeReason: Old 'why are you picking on me' vs new 'when did the whole room turn against me'; JPN 俺包囲網 = 'encirclement against me,' a military metaphor.
